faqs.q: Why does lithium need regular blood tests?
faqs.a: The helpful range and the unsafe range sit fairly close together, so regular blood tests keep the level where it should be. The checks also keep an eye on the kidneys and thyroid, which lithium can affect over time.

faqs.q: How long does lithium take to work?
faqs.a: Lithium works gradually. Some benefit can show during an acute episode, but the steadier, protective effect builds over weeks of consistent use.

faqs.q: Can I stop taking lithium on my own?
faqs.a: No. People should never raise, lower, or stop lithium on their own, and stopping suddenly can trigger a relapse. Any change goes through the prescriber.
